title: Route Marker Tabs
prompt:
Create a production-ready Route Marker Tabs pattern using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original navigation component presents three route phases—Plan, Move, and Verify—with a small amber marker moving between labels. Selecting a tab updates a concise panel beneath it, making the active phase understandable from both the selected tab and the written panel title. Use a light Bento composition: #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a gentle neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary text. Amber is the only bright accent and no card surface may use a gradient.

Use tablist, tab, tabpanel, aria-selected, aria-controls, aria-labelledby, and a polite live confirmation. Support ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Home, and End keyboard navigation in addition to click, Enter, and Space. Every tab must have a 44px minimum target and visible 3px focus ring. Move the marker with a spring using stiffness 350 and damping 27; fade the panel copy in over 150ms ease-out. Use deterministic local state only; do not introduce timers, random values, storage, remote data, network calls, or page-level positioning.

At 320px, keep all three labels readable in one compact row; at larger sizes, allow a wider panel. Preserve logical focus order, AA contrast, and textual selection cues in addition to the marker. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by updating the marker immediately and keeping only a short opacity fade. Return full paste-ready client code, a runnable pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:
*/ .route-tabs{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);max-width:340px;border-bottom:4px solid #f5e642}.route-tabs button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;color:#111;font-weight:800}.route-tabs button[aria-selected=true]{background:#f4f5f6}.route-tabs button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.route-tabs *{transition:none}}
